scenarios.opening_query: The state suspended my foster-parent certification over an allegation that was withdrawn the next day.
scenarios.hidden_fact: They also removed our foster daughter of two years during the suspension — getting her placement back through the dependency court is the fight that matters most.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They removed our foster daughter of two years during the suspension — her placement is the fight that matters.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a child's placement is at stake beyond the certification
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No child is placed with us right now — it's purely the certification record, and the withdrawn allegation should never have triggered suspension.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No placement right now — it's the certification record itself.

scenarios.opening_query: My CNA license renewal is stuck because of a 'flag' nobody at the board can explain.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The flag is a discipline record from a state I've never worked in — someone used my license number and identity there; clearing the identity theft is the unlock.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The flag is discipline from a state I've never worked in — someone used my identity and number there.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether professional identity theft underlies the flag
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The flag is mine — an old diversion agreement I completed; the board just never recorded completion, and the record correction is the ask.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The flag's mine — a completed diversion the board never recorded. Fix the record.

scenarios.opening_query: Our church's food pantry got shut down by an inspector over rules we've never heard of.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > General (Local/Municipal) || Business and Corporate > Non-Profit
scenarios.hidden_fact: The citation hinges on our nonprofit status paperwork — our 501(c)(3) food-handling exemption lapsed when a filing was missed; restoring the nonprofit compliance stack reopens us.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It hinges on our lapsed 501(c)(3) filings — restore the nonprofit compliance stack and we reopen.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether nonprofit-status lapses drive the closure
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Our filings are current — the inspector applied restaurant rules to a charity pantry, and the administrative appeal is the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Filings are current — he applied restaurant rules to a charity pantry. Appeal it.

scenarios.opening_query: The VA reduced my disability rating right after my condition got demonstrably worse.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Military/Veterans || Criminal Law > Expunction
scenarios.hidden_fact: The reduction letter cites a 'fugitive felon' flag — an ancient bench warrant from a case dismissed decades ago that never got cleared from the court record; quashing and sealing that record restores the rating automatically.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The letter cites a 'fugitive felon' flag — an ancient bench warrant from a long-dismissed case. Clear that court record and the rating restores automatically.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a stale court record triggers the benefits flag
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No flags — it's a genuine rating-reduction decision after their review exam, and the VA appeal with new medical evidence is the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No flags — it's a real rating reduction after their exam, and the appeal with new evidence is the fight.
scenarios.vetting_note: Fugitive-felon-flag mechanics are real and obscure; record relief is the unlock.

scenarios.opening_query: My daughter's college financial aid got clawed back mid-semester and she may have to withdraw.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > Student Rights || Administrative Law > General (Federal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a federal Pell/verification dispute — Education Department rules and their appeal process govern, not anything the college controls.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a federal Pell verification dispute — Education Department rules and appeals govern, not the college.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which entity's rules govern the aid decision
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The college itself did it — they reclassified her residency status arbitrarily, and the school's own appeal process with due-process questions is the venue.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The college did it — arbitrary residency reclassification, so it's the school's process and due process.

scenarios.opening_query: The state garnished my tax refund for a college debt from twenty years ago that I don't recognize.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a defaulted state-guaranteed student loan — the student-loan rehabilitation and dispute machinery is the route, with its own defenses and statute questions.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a defaulted state-guaranteed student loan — the loan rehabilitation and dispute machinery is the route.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the offset stems from student-loan default
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's not a loan — it's a tuition 'overaward' the university reported to the state; the agency-offset appeal is the route.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Not a loan — a tuition 'overaward' the university reported. Appeal the offset.
